prompt:
The supply room logs restocks in flat ledgers ‚Äî one `category.item=count` per line ‚Äî and we keep answering 'how many beakers do we have' by eyeballing the file. Write `stockrep.zsh` in the repo root, run as `zsh stockrep.zsh <ledger> [first PATTERN | count PATTERN]`, zsh 5.9 native, with a `typeset -A` associative array as the working store. Hard requirement: ZERO external commands ‚Äî no sort, no awk, no grep; the acceptance harness runs the script with an EMPTY PATH, so any fork fails loudly. zsh covers all of it natively: `${(ko)map}` for ascending key order, `${(o)arr}` for sorting plain arrays, subscript flags for the lookups.

Ledger grammar, line by line:
- Blank lines and lines starting with `#` are skipped.
- A data line is `category.item=count`: the category is everything up to the FIRST dot; the item may contain further dots (`glass.tall.jar` is category `glass`, item `tall.jar`). Neither side of that first dot may be empty, the key may not contain whitespace or `=`, and the count must be digits only. Counts are decimal even with leading zeros ‚Äî `ink.cart=015` is 15 units, not octal.
- The same key may appear on several lines; quantities ACCUMULATE.
- Every other non-skipped line is malformed: keep it, byte-for-byte raw.
- The last line still counts when the file has no trailing newline.

Default mode (ledger argument only) prints the report to stdout, byte-exact, all four sections always present even when empty:
- `CATEGORIES` ‚Äî one `category<TAB>kinds<TAB>units` line per category (kinds = number of distinct keys in it, units = summed quantities), ascending byte order.
- `ITEMS` ‚Äî one `key<TAB>quantity` line per distinct key, ascending byte order.
- `MALFORMED` ‚Äî the raw malformed lines, ascending byte order.
- `TOTALS` ‚Äî exactly four lines: `categories<TAB>N`, `items<TAB>N`, `units<TAB>N`, `malformed<TAB>N`, in that order.
Section names are bare uppercase words on their own lines. All ordering must come from zsh's own flags ‚Äî there is no sort(1) on an empty PATH.

`first PATTERN` ‚Äî the pattern-subscript lookup: print `key<TAB>quantity` for a key matching PATTERN. This is exactly what `${map[(i)PATTERN]}` is for ‚Äî an associative-array subscript with the (i) flag is matched as a PATTERN against the keys, so `first '*.flask'` finds `glass.flask`. If no key matches: `stockrep.zsh: no item matches: <PATTERN>` on stderr, exit 1.

`count PATTERN` ‚Äî print the number of matching keys on the first line (`"${(@k)map[(I)PATTERN]}"` hands you all of them), then every matching `key<TAB>quantity` line in ascending key order. Zero matches is not an error: print `0` and exit 0.

Exit contract (all errors go to stderr, stdout stays empty):
- no arguments at all: just the usage line, exit 64.
- ledger missing or unreadable: `stockrep.zsh: cannot read: <arg>`, exit 66.
- unknown mode: `stockrep.zsh: unknown mode: <mode>`, then the usage line, exit 64.
- `first`/`count` without exactly one pattern argument: `stockrep.zsh: wrong number of arguments`, then the usage line, exit 64.
Usage line, byte-exact: `usage: stockrep.zsh <ledger> [first PATTERN | count PATTERN]`

Everything runs under LC_ALL=C. Acceptance: `zsh test_assocrep.zsh` from the workspace root must pass unmodified; it builds its own fixture ledgers. Do not modify the test file.
